hints.to bind: That would be 'vincire' — 'Vincet' comes from 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.to avenge: That would be 'vindicare' — 'Vincet' comes from 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.to live: That would be 'vivere' — 'Vincet' comes from 'vincere' (to conquer)

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'vincit' — 'vincet' with -e- is future indicative (3rd conj.)
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'vincat' — 'vincet' is future indicative
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'vicit' — 'vincet' is future indicative

hints.vincio, vincire, vinxi, vinctum: These are the parts of 'vincire' (to bind/fetter)
hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live)
hints.vindico, vindicare, vindicavi, vindicatu: These are the parts of 'vindicare' (to avenge/claim)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'amorem' — 'amor' is nominative, the subject
hints.vocative: Vocative is also 'amor' in 3rd decl., but here it is the nominative subject

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'amoris' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'amoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'amoris' ends in -is

steps.reveal: patriae = of the fatherland (gen. sg., with 'amor')

hints.dative: Dative is also 'patriae', but here it is genitive — love 'of' the fatherland
hints.nominative: Nominative plural is also 'patriae', but here it is gen. sg. with 'amor'
hints.vocative: Vocative would be 'patria' — 'patriae' is genitive singular

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'patriae' ends in -ae (1st declension)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'patriae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension has genitive in -ei — 'patriae' ends in -ae

hints.accusative: Accusative plural would be 'laudes' — 'laudum' with -um is genitive plural
hints.ablative: Ablative plural would be 'laudibus' — 'laudum' is genitive plural
hints.dative: Dative plural would be 'laudibus' — 'laudum' is genitive plural

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'laudis' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'laudis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'laudis' ends in -is

steps.reveal: cupido = desire (nom. sg., second subject with 'amor')

hints.ablative: Ablative would be 'cupidine' — 'cupido' is nominative singular
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'cupidinem' — 'cupido' is nominative, subject of 'vincet'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'cupidinis' ends in -is (3rd declension)
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'cupidinis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'cupidinis' ends in -is

translation: Love of country will prevail, and the boundless desire for glory
